name: Debug session
description: Guided troubleshooting from symptoms to root cause and verification steps.

placeholders.name: environment
placeholders.description: OS, runtime versions, deployment target, or IDE.

placeholders.name: symptoms
placeholders.description: What fails, error messages, stack traces, or flaky behavior.

placeholders.name: reproduction
placeholders.description: Steps or commands to reproduce, or 'not reliably reproducible'.

expected_output_format.description: Answer as a debug playbook the engineer can execute; use markdown checklists where useful.

examples.title: Flaky test in CI

placeholder_values.symptoms: Integration test `test_upload_queue` fails ~1 in 20 runs with `Connection reset by peer` when talking to local MinIO.
placeholder_values.recent_changes: Bumped `urllib3` from 2.0 to 2.2; added parallel test execution (`pytest -n auto`).

examples.sample_response:
## Problem restatement
Parallel tests race against a shared MinIO bucket or exhaust connection pools, causing intermittent resets.

## Hypotheses (ranked)
1. Shared bucket/prefix collisions under `pytest-xdist`.
2. `urllib3` connection pooling + server `keep-alive` limits.
3. MinIO container resource limits in CI.

## Isolation steps
- [ ] Run the single test serially 100 times; if stable, concurrency is implicated.
- [ ] Give each worker a unique bucket via `worker_id` fixture.
- [ ] Capture tcpdump or MinIO logs on failure.

## Most likely root cause
Prefix collisions when multiple workers write the same object keys.

## Fix or workaround
Namespace keys with `worker_id` or use temporary buckets per session.

## How to verify the fix
Stress run with `-n 8 --count=200` green; add a guard assert on key prefix uniqueness.

## Prevention
Document storage test isolation; add a CI job that runs integration tests in parallel only after bucket isolation is enforced.

examples.title: Local dev server 500

placeholder_values.symptoms: Every `/api/health` returns 500; terminal shows `TypeError: fetch failed` in middleware only on cold start.
placeholder_values.recent_changes: Added edge middleware that calls an internal HTTP URL using `localhost` instead of `127.0.0.1`.
placeholder_values.reproduction: Stop server, `rm -rf .next`, `npm run dev`, first request to `/api/health` fails; second succeeds.

examples.sample_response:
## Problem restatement
Cold-start middleware cannot reach internal API over `localhost` in the edge runtime.

## Hypotheses (ranked)
1. Edge runtime DNS/IPv6 resolution differs for `localhost`.
2. Server not listening until after first request.

## Isolation steps
- [ ] Replace `localhost` with `127.0.0.1` or use relative `fetch('/api/...')`.
- [ ] Add timing logs around middleware execution.

## Most likely root cause
Edge middleware resolves `localhost` differently on first invocation.

## Fix or workaround
Use same-origin relative URLs or explicit IPv4 loopback.

## How to verify the fix
Cold start test script hits health ten times in a row.

## Prevention
Lint rule or doc note: no `localhost` in edge middleware.
